name: Businessolver Benefits Administration API
what_it_does: Businessolver benefits administration REST API for employee benefits enrollment, eligibility management, and carrier data exchange. Enables AI agents to manage open enrollment workflows and employee benefits elections for HR operations automation, handle benefits eligibility determination and life event changes for benefits administration, access carrier EDI data exchange and enrollment file transmission for insurance carrier integration, retrieve benefits cost modeling and plan comparison data for employee decision support, manage COBRA administration and qualifying event processing for benefits continuation compliance, handle ACA compliance tracking and reporting for employer mandate compliance, access HSA/FSA/HRA account administration for consumer-directed benefits management, retrieve dependent verification and eligibility audit data for benefits fraud prevention, manage benefits appeals and exception processing for HR case management, and integrate Businessolver with HRIS, payroll, and carrier systems.
use_cases: Processing open enrollment from benefits administration agents
Managing life event changes from HR operations agents
Transmitting carrier enrollment from benefits data exchange agents
Tracking ACA compliance from employer mandate agents
not_for: Small businesses without formal benefits administration
Individual consumer benefits without employer sponsorship
Non-US benefits without American benefits plan structure
best_when: A mid-to-large employer, benefits broker, or HR outsourcing firm wanting AI agents to automate open enrollment, life event processing, carrier data exchange, COBRA administration, and ACA compliance for employee benefits programs.
avoid_when: ACA EMPLOYER MANDATE COMPLIANCE: Automated ACA affordability calculations must use current IRS safe harbor percentage and plan cost data; automated ACA reporting (Forms 1094-C/1095-C) with incorrect FTE count or affordability determination creates IRS penalty exposure. HIPAA PHI in benefits data — benefits eligibility and enrollment data contains protected health information; automated carrier EDI transmission requires HIPAA-compliant data handling, BAA with carriers, and minimum necessary access controls. COBRA qualifying event timing — automated COBRA election notice generation must meet ERISA 14-day notice and 60-day election period requirements; automated timing errors create COBRA continuation plan liability. Dependent verification — automated dependent eligibility without verification creates benefits fraud exposure; implement dependent verification before automated enrollment to prevent fraudulent coverage.

auth.notes: Businessolver uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON and EDI support. Des Moines, Iowa HQ. Founded 1998. Private (ABRY Partners PE). Benefits administration SaaS. 1,000+ employer clients. 4M+ employees administered. ACA, COBRA, and carrier EDI capabilities. Competes with Benefitfocus, bswift, and PlanSource for benefits administration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — PEPM enterprise subscription
pricing.notes: Des Moines, Iowa. ABRY Partners PE-backed. Founded 1998. 1,000+ employer clients. 4M+ employees. Benefits administration specialist. Competes with Benefitfocus and bswift.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; open enrollment has strict SLA during peak periods

known_agent_gotchas: ACA EMPLOYER MANDATE ACCURACY: Automated ACA affordability and FTE calculation must use current IRS safe harbor percentage; automated 1094-C/1095-C reporting with incorrect affordability determination creates IRS 4980H penalty exposure — validate ACA parameters against current IRS guidance each tax year

HIPAA PHI minimum necessary — benefits eligibility and enrollment data is PHI; automated carrier EDI must apply minimum necessary standard; agent access scope must be limited to required enrollment data only, not full health record

COBRA timing criticality — automated COBRA notice generation must meet exact ERISA timing (qualifying event notice within 30 days, election notice within 14 days, 60-day election window); automated timing error creates plan liability

Open enrollment concurrency — peak open enrollment periods create high API load; automated agents submitting elections simultaneously may encounter rate limits; implement exponential backoff and queue management
Carrier EDI file validation — automated carrier enrollment file generation must pass carrier-specific 834 EDI validation; EDI errors cause carrier enrollment failures affecting employee coverage

Dependent eligibility verification — automated dependent enrollment without eligibility verification creates benefits fraud exposure; implement verification workflow before finalizing automated enrollment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Benefits administration. SOC2, HIPAA. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Employee benefits and PHI data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Benefits administration. SOC2, HIPAA. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Employee benefits and PHI.
